message:
perf(pager): short-circuit ShardedPageCache empty-shards walk on clear

`ShardedPageCache::clear / enable_fast_path / disable_fast_path` walked
all 128 shard mutexes unconditionally. The flat-slot front-cache absorbs
every admit until probe-length overflow (32 chains), so on the bench
shape — `Connection::open_with_env_and_pager` →
`refresh_committed_state` → `cache.clear()` per worker open — every
clear paid for 128 mutex acquires against shards that had never seen an
insert.

Add `shards_dirty: AtomicBool`, set by the three overflow-insert sites
after the shard lock is released, and consumed via `swap(false, AcqRel)`
at the top of clear / enable_fast_path / disable_fast_path. The flag is
conservative — a concurrent insert ordered after our swap re-arms it
for the next clear, and an insert ordered before our swap is visible to
the subsequent shard walk. The pattern mirrors `FlatPageSlots::clear`'s
existing `count == 0 && !had_tombstones` short-circuit.

Microbench `bench_sharded_cache_clear_empty_shards_microbench`
(`cargo test -p fsqlite-pager --lib --release -- --ignored --nocapture
bench_sharded_cache_clear_empty_shards_microbench`) does paired runs
in one binary by toggling `shards_dirty.store(true)` to force the
128-shard walk on the baseline pass:

  baseline 529 ns/clear → optimized 5 ns/clear (~106x)

The 524 ns saved on the empty path covers the 0.63 % MT8 inclusive
self-time the symbol owned in
`tests/artifacts/perf/profiling-mt-mvcc-20260424T161631Z/perf_mt8_callgraph.txt`
under the `Connection::open_with_env_and_pager` chain.

All 85 `page_cache::tests` pass; `cargo clippy -p fsqlite-pager` and
`cargo fmt --all -- --check` clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
